text:
Confirmed. `uniform_boundedness_principle` is the body `banach_steinhaus h` — an exact ℝ-specialization of Mathlib's `banach_steinhaus`, adding nothing.

TAUCETI-VERDICT-caf322704f730c40dfe9163c

```json
{
  "verdict": "block",
  "summary": "uniform_boundedness_principle is a thin ℝ-specialized wrapper whose proof is literally `banach_steinhaus h`, directly replaceable by Mathlib's `banach_steinhaus`. IsCompactOrientedManifold adds nothing over `CompactSpace` since its `oriented` field is `True`.",
  "findings": [
    { "file": "TauCeti/AtlasExamples.lean", "line": "42",
      "issue": "uniform_boundedness_principle is `banach_steinhaus h` verbatim, specialized to scalar ℝ. It is a strict special case of the existing Mathlib theorem and provides no added API.",
      "fix": "Delete it and use `banach_steinhaus` directly.",
      "evidence": "mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/BanachSteinhaus.lean:36 `theorem banach_steinhaus ... (h : ∀ x, ∃ C, ∀ i, ‖g i x‖ ≤ C) : ∃ C', ∀ i, ‖g i‖ ≤ C'`; the PR proof is `:= banach_steinhaus h`." },
    { "file": "TauCeti/AtlasExamples.lean", "line": "26",
      "issue": "IsCompactOrientedManifold's only content is `compact : CompactSpace M` (the `oriented` field is `True`, i.e. vacuous), so the class and `IsCompactOrientedManifold.isCompact` duplicate `CompactSpace` with a renamed field.",
      "fix": "Drop the class; use `CompactSpace M` directly, or build on Mathlib's actual orientation API rather than a `True` placeholder.",
      "evidence": "Diff: `oriented : True`; `isCompact` is just `h.compact`. Mathlib provides `CompactSpace` and orientation typeclasses already." }
  ]
}
```

verdict_obj.summary: uniform_boundedness_principle is a thin ℝ-specialized wrapper whose proof is literally `banach_steinhaus h`, directly replaceable by Mathlib's `banach_steinhaus`. IsCompactOrientedManifold adds nothing over `CompactSpace` since its `oriented` field is `True`.

findings.issue: uniform_boundedness_principle is `banach_steinhaus h` verbatim, specialized to scalar ℝ. It is a strict special case of the existing Mathlib theorem and provides no added API.
findings.evidence: mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/BanachSteinhaus.lean:36 `theorem banach_steinhaus ... (h : ∀ x, ∃ C, ∀ i, ‖g i x‖ ≤ C) : ∃ C', ∀ i, ‖g i‖ ≤ C'`; the PR proof is `:= banach_steinhaus h`.

findings.issue: IsCompactOrientedManifold's only content is `compact : CompactSpace M` (the `oriented` field is `True`, i.e. vacuous), so the class and `IsCompactOrientedManifold.isCompact` duplicate `CompactSpace` with a renamed field.
findings.fix: Drop the class; use `CompactSpace M` directly, or build on Mathlib's actual orientation API rather than a `True` placeholder.
findings.evidence: Diff: `oriented : True`; `isCompact` is just `h.compact`. Mathlib provides `CompactSpace` and orientation typeclasses already.
